concepts.title: Refund Window
concepts.why: Defines refund eligibility and the approval path before any refund action.
concepts.summary: Customers are eligible for a standard refund within 30 days of the captured payment date. Refunds after 30 days require finance approval, and fraud-related refunds require risk review.
caveats: Enterprise contracts can override the standard refund window.
The policy does not decide refund amount; it only decides approval path.
The agent must cite the support ticket or contract note before recommending a refund.

concepts.title: Refund Payment
concepts.why: Defines the endpoint and required inputs for creating a refund request.
concepts.summary: Creates a refund request for a captured payment. Requires payment_id, amount, reason_code, and support_ticket_id.
caveats: Refunds reduce Net Revenue based on refund effective date.
Refunds outside the standard window require manual approval.
The agent must not create a refund without a support ticket citation.

concepts.title: Support Policy Manual
concepts.why: Provides source support for refund-window and trial-policy claims.
concepts.summary: Standard refunds are available within 30 days of captured payment. Refunds after 30 days require finance approval. Fraud-related refunds require risk review.
caveats: This is a fictional source for demonstration. In a real OKF bundle, this file should identify the policy owner and approval workflow.
citations: Fictional support policy manual for the OKF demo bundle.

concepts.title: Billing API Docs
concepts.why: Provides source support for refund API behavior and required fields.
concepts.summary: Refunds require a captured payment identifier and a support ticket identifier.
caveats: This is a fictional source for demonstration. In a real OKF bundle, this file should link to the live source document, commit, ticket, or exported documentation snapshot.
citations: Fictional billing API documentation for the OKF demo bundle.

warnings.message: This bundle uses fictional sources and placeholder URLs.
